intentional_test_features: Acute-on-chronic worsening of intermittent claudication: 6 months of exertional calf cramp, worse over a week - the AI Doctor must decide whether this is stable claudication (viable limb) or a limb-threatening escalation, without being able to palpate pulses remotely

Over-triage vs under-triage balance: an asymptomatic-at-rest, viable limb with reproducible exertional pain relieved by rest is stable PAD needing urgent outpatient vascular work-up, not necessarily an emergency - but acute limb ischaemia (the 6 Ps) and DVT must be actively excluded and safety-netted
Underlying severe hyperlipidaemia: physical stigmata (tendon xanthomas, xanthelasma, corneal arcus) point to a severe lipid disorder driving systemic atherosclerosis and very high cardiovascular risk
Mimic discrimination: exertional calf pain relieved by rest (claudication) versus DVT (constant, unilateral swelling) versus neurogenic claudication (relieved by spinal flexion/sitting)
Global vascular risk: carotid bruits and absent pedal pulses signal widespread atherosclerosis, so antiplatelet/statin therapy and risk-factor modification are as important as the leg symptoms

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-03)
